back_translation: And the whole congregation lifted up and gave their voice; and the people wept that night.
And all the sons of Israel grumbled against Moses and against Aaron; and the whole congregation said to them: 'If only we had died in the land of Egypt, or in this wilderness, if only we had died!'
And why is the LORD bringing us into this land to fall by the sword? Our wives and our little ones will become plunder. Is it not good for us to return to Egypt?
And they said, a man to his brother: 'Let us appoint a head (leader) and let us return to Egypt.'
And Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before the whole assembly of the congregation of the sons of Israel.
And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, from those who spied out the land, tore their garments.
And they said to the whole congregation of the sons of Israel, saying: 'The land that we passed through to spy it out, exceedingly exceedingly good is the land.'
If the LORD delights in us, He will bring us to this land and give it to us — a land which flows with milk and honey.

Only do not rebel against the LORD, and you, do not fear the people of the land, for they are bread for us. Their shadow (protection) has departed from over them, and the LORD is with us. Do not fear them.
But the whole congregation said to stone them with stones; and the glory of the LORD appeared in the Tent of Meeting to all the sons of Israel.
And the LORD said to Moses: 'How long will this people despise Me? And how long will they not believe in Me, in all the signs which I have done in the midst of him?'
I will strike him with a plague and dispossess him; and I will make you a nation greater and mightier than he.
And Moses said to the LORD: 'And Egypt will hear, because You brought up by Your strength this people from their midst.'

And they will say to the inhabitants of this land — they have heard that You, O LORD, are in the midst of this people; that eye to eye You appear, O LORD, and Your cloud stands over them, and in a pillar of cloud You...
If You kill this people as one man, then the nations who have heard Your fame will say, saying:
'From the inability of the LORD to bring this people to the land which He swore to them, He slaughtered them in the wilderness.'
And now, may the power of my Lord be magnified, please, as You have spoken, saying:

'The LORD is long of nose (slow to anger) and abundant of steadfast love, bearing iniquity and transgression; and acquitting, He does not acquit; visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon sons, upon the third and upon the fourth (generation).'
Forgive, please, the iniquity of this people, according to the greatness of Your steadfast love, and as You have borne this people from Egypt until here.
And the LORD said: 'I have forgiven, according to your word.'
But as I live, and the glory of the LORD shall fill all the earth,
for all the men who have seen My glory and My signs which I have done in Egypt and in the wilderness, and have tested Me these ten times, and have not listened to My voice —
if they shall see the land which I swore to their fathers, and all who despise Me shall not see it.
But My servant Caleb, because another spirit has been with him, and he has followed after Me — I will bring him to the land where he entered, and his seed shall inherit it.
And the Amalekite and the Canaanite are dwelling in the valley; tomorrow turn and set out for yourselves toward the wilderness, the way of the Reed Sea (Red Sea).
And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:
'How long for this wicked congregation, who are grumbling against Me? The grumblings of the sons of Israel, which they are grumbling against Me, I have heard.'
Say to them: 'As I live, declares the LORD, surely as you have spoken in My ears, so I will do to you.'
In this wilderness your bodies shall fall, and all those counted of you, by all your number, from a son of twenty years and upward, who have grumbled against Me.
Surely you shall not enter the land which I lifted up My hand to settle you in it, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.
But your little ones, of whom you said they would be plunder — I will bring them, and they shall know the land which you have rejected in it.
But your bodies, you — they shall fall in this wilderness.
And your sons shall be shepherds in the wilderness for forty years, and they shall bear your harlotries (unfaithfulness) until the end of your bodies in the wilderness.

According to the number of days which you spied out the land, forty days, a day for the year, a day for the year, you shall bear your iniquities, forty years; and you shall know My alienation (turning away).
I, the LORD, have spoken; if I do not do this to all this wicked congregation, those who are gathered against Me — in this wilderness they shall be consumed, and there they shall die.
And the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land, and they returned, and made the whole congregation grumble against him, by bringing out an evil report against the land —
and the men who brought out the evil report of the land died in a plague before the LORD.
And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh lived, from those men who went to spy out the land.
And Moses spoke these words to all the sons of Israel; and the people mourned greatly.
And they rose early in the morning, and went up to the top of the mountain, saying: 'Here we are, and we will go up to the place which the LORD has said, for we have sinned.'
And Moses said: 'Why is this — you are transgressing the word (mouth) of the LORD? And it will not succeed.'
Do not go up, for the LORD is not in your midst; and you shall not be struck before your enemies.
For the Amalekite and the Canaanite are there before you, and you shall fall by the sword; for because of this you have turned back from following the LORD, and the LORD will not be with you.
But they presumed to go up to the top of the mountain; and the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and Moses, did not depart from the midst of the camp.
And the Amalekite and the Canaanite who were dwelling in that mountain came down, and struck them, and beat them down as far as Hormah.
